UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a customer's profile, linked to an external authentication system. This entity stores personal information and preferences, ensuring data requiring secure access is clearly defined.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier from the external authentication system, linking this profile to an authenticated user. (Relationship: ExternalAuthSystem 1:1 UserProfile)

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user for communication purposes. This is not used for authentication.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the user.

defaultShippingAddressId.description: Reference to the user's preferred default shipping address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

defaultBillingAddressId.description: Reference to the user's preferred default billing address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address associated with a user profile, used for shipping and billing purposes. Access to this data requires user authorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the user profile this address belongs to, ensuring ownership and access control. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (e.g., apartment number, suite).

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal or ZIP code of the address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the address was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the address was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase transaction, containing details about the order. Access to order history and details is sensitive and requires authorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the user profile who placed the order, establishing customer ownership. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including shipping and taxes.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the shipping address used for this order. This stores the specific address chosen at the time of order. (Relationship: Order 1:1 Address)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the billing address used for this order. This stores the specific address chosen at the time of order. (Relationship: Order 1:1 Address)

paymentStatus.description: The status of the payment for this order (e.g., 'paid', 'refunded', 'failed').

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number provided by the shipping carrier for the order shipment.

shippingCarrier.description: The name of the shipping carrier used for the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an individual product item within a specific order. This details what was purchased.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the product that was purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

priceAtPurchase.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of purchase, for historical accuracy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product available for sale in the Bonanza store. Product data forms a core part of the application's content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

stockQuantity.description: The number of units of this product currently in stock.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to high-quality images of the product. These images may be enhanced using Generative AI.

categoryIds.description: References to categories this product belongs to. (Relationship: Product N:N Category)

weight.description: The weight of the product in a standard unit (e.g., grams, ounces).

dimensions.description: The physical dimensions of the product, e.g., '10x5x2 cm'.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for internal inventory management.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if the product should be prominently featured on the website.

isActive.description: A flag indicating if the product is currently active and available for sale.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification or grouping for products, such as 'Perfume' or 'Maquillaje', enabling organized navigation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category for visual display.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to the parent category for hierarchical categorization (e.g., 'Tratamiento' might be a parent to 'Abeille Royale'). (Relationship: Category 1:N Category - self-referencing)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was last updated.

ProductReview.title: ProductReview
ProductReview.description: Represents a customer's review for a specific product, including a rating and comment. Reviews often require moderation, making this entity part of content security.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductReview entity.

productId.description: Reference to the product being reviewed. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductReview)

userProfileId.description: Reference to the user profile who submitted the review. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ProductReview)

rating.description: The rating given by the user, typically on a scale of 1 to 5.

comment.description: The textual comment or feedback provided by the user.

reviewDate.description: The date and time when the review was submitted.

isApproved.description: A flag indicating if the review has been approved by a moderator for public display.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the review record was last updated.

PromotionalContent.title: PromotionalContent
PromotionalContent.description: Represents various types of promotional content displayed on the website, such as hero banners, product showcases, or carousels. This enables dynamic updates of marketing material.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PromotionalContent entity.

title.description: The main title of the promotional content.

subtitle.description: A secondary title or brief descriptor for the content.

description.description: A more detailed description or body text for the promotional content.

imageUrl.description: URL to the primary image associated with the promotional content.

callToActionText.description: The text for the call-to-action button (e.g., 'Shop Now', 'Discover More').

callToActionUrl.description: The URL to which the call-to-action button links.

displayLocation.description: Indicates where on the website this content should be displayed (e.g., 'hero_section', 'top_banner', 'product_showcase_abeille_royale', 'product_carousel').

startDate.description: The date and time when the promotional content becomes active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the promotional content expires.

isActive.description: A flag indicating if the promotional content is currently active and visible on the website.

orderIndex.description: An integer defining the display order of the content, especially within carousels or lists.

theme.description: The visual theme associated with the content, e.g., 'light', 'dark', which influences styling.

productIds.description: References to products featured in this promotional content, if applicable (e.g., for a product carousel). (Relationship: PromotionalContent N:N Product)

gradient.description: CSS gradient string for background styling, often used in dynamic hero sections.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the promotional content record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the promotional content record was last updated.

NewsletterSubscriber.title: NewsletterSubscriber
NewsletterSubscriber.description: Represents a user who has subscribed to receive email newsletters and marketing communications. Email addresses are sensitive data requiring secure handling.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsletterSubscriber entity.

email.description: The email address of the subscriber.

subscriptionDate.description: The date and time when the user subscribed.

isConfirmed.description: A flag indicating if the subscription has been confirmed (e.g., via a double opt-in process).

unsubscribeDate.description: The date and time when the user unsubscribed from the newsletter.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscriber record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscriber record was last updated.

ContactSubmission.title: ContactSubmission
ContactSubmission.description: Represents a message submitted through the website's contact form. This data may contain user queries or feedback, requiring proper handling and access control.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactSubmission entity.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the contact form.

email.description: The email address of the person submitting the contact form.

subject.description: The subject line of the contact message.

message.description: The full content of the message submitted.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the contact message was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the contact request (e.g., 'new', 'in_progress', 'resolved').

assignedTo.description: Reference to an internal agent or team responsible for handling this submission.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the contact submission record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the contact submission record was last updated.

ExclusiveExperience.title: ExclusiveExperience
ExclusiveExperience.description: Represents unique services, spa treatments, or special events offered by Bonanza. This content requires management and potential booking functionality.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExclusiveExperience entity.

title.description: The title or name of the exclusive experience (e.g., 'Spas', 'Servicios').

description.description: A detailed description of what the experience entails.

shortDescription.description: A brief summary of the experience, suitable for listings or previews.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the exclusive experience.

callToActionText.description: The text for the call-to-action button related to the experience (e.g., 'Book Now', 'Learn More').

callToActionUrl.description: The URL to which the call-to-action button for the experience links.

price.description: The cost of the exclusive experience, if applicable.

duration.description: The typical duration of the experience (e.g., '60 minutes', 'Full day').

location.description: The physical or virtual location where the experience takes place (e.g., 'Online', 'Paris Boutique').

isAvailable.description: A flag indicating if the experience is currently available for booking or participation.

bookingLink.description: A direct link to book the experience, if separate from the main callToActionUrl.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the exclusive experience record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the exclusive experience record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid` must match `{userProfileId}`). The `externalAuthUserId` field matches `{userProfileId}` from the path, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores physical addresses for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid` must match the parent `{userProfileId}`). The `userProfileId` field is denormalized in each document to match the parent path's `{userProfileId}`, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier of the address.

definition.description: Stores purchase orders for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid` must match the parent `{userProfileId}`). The `userProfileId` field is denormalized in each document to match the parent path's `{userProfileId}`, ensuring Authorization Independence and supporting QAPs for list operations.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a specific order. Access is restricted to the owner of the parent order (`request.auth.uid` must match the grandparent `{userProfileId}`). The `orderId` field is denormalized to match the parent path's `{orderId}`. For full Authorization Independence, `userProfileId` should ideally also be denormalized into this document.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Contains details of all available products. Publicly readable by anyone. Writes are restricted to administrators. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (public read / admin write).

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Defines product categories for navigation. Publicly readable by anyone. Writes are restricted to administrators. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (public read / admin write).

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

definition.description: Stores reviews for products. Publicly readable only if `isApproved` is true. Writable only by the `userProfileId` specified in the document (which must match `request.auth.uid`) or by administrators. The `productId` and `userProfileId` fields are denormalized for Authorization Independence and QAPs.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

definition.description: Manages various promotional banners and content sections. Publicly readable by anyone. Writes are restricted to administrators. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (public read / admin write).

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the promotional content.

definition.description: Stores email subscriptions for newsletters. Users can only create new subscriptions. Reads, updates, and deletes are restricted to administrators. Supports Structural Segregation by intent.

params.name: subscriberId
params.description: The unique identifier for the newsletter subscriber (e.g., hash of email or a unique ID).

definition.description: Records messages submitted through the contact form. Anyone (authenticated or unauthenticated) can create a submission. Reads, updates, and deletes are restricted to administrators. Supports Structural Segregation by intent.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact submission.

definition.description: Details unique services and events. Publicly readable by anyone. Writes are restricted to administrators. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (public read / admin write).

params.name: experienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exclusive experience.

definition.description: This collection determines administrative roles using 'Existence over Content'. If a document exists at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}`, the user is an admin. Documents typically contain minimal data, serving solely for role checking. Access to this collection is restricted to administrators.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has administrator privileges (must match `request.auth.uid`).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure robust, debuggable, and scalable security rules.  

**Authorization Independence:**
*   For all user-specific and user-owned data (UserProfile, Address, Order, OrderItem), the `userProfileId` is either directly embedded in the collection path (e.g., `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`) or explicitly denormalized within the document itself and also mirrored in a parent path segment (e.g., `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/addresses/{addressId}` where `address.userProfileId` must match `{userProfileId}`). This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to verify ownership, enabling atomic operations and simplifying debugging. Specifically for `OrderItem`, while its schema only contains `orderId`, for full authorization independence, `userProfileId` would ideally also be present in each `OrderItem` document to directly link it to the owner without traversing up to the `Order` document via `get()`. However, the current nested path structure (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`) still allows rules to efficiently check `request.auth.uid == userProfileId` from the path for ownership.
*   For `ProductReview`, the `userProfileId` field is included in the document, allowing direct ownership verification (`resource.data.userProfileId == request.auth.uid`) without `get()` calls.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
*   Publicly viewable content (Products, Categories, Promotional Content, Exclusive Experiences) is stored in top-level collections (e.g., `/products`, `/categories`), ensuring all documents within these collections share a common 'public read' security posture. This simplifies rules significantly as there's no need for complex conditional logic based on document content for public access.
*   User-private data is segregated into subcollections under a user-specific path (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/addresses`, `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/orders`). All documents within these subcollections intrinsically belong to the `{userProfileId}` in the path.
*   Admin-managed data (Newsletter Subscribers, Contact Submissions) are in dedicated collections. While users can *create* new `NewsletterSubscriber` and `ContactSubmission` entries, only administrators have read/update/delete access, clearly segregating management responsibilities.
*   An explicit `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection is used for global administrator roles, leveraging 'Existence over Content' for role-based access control without custom claims.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   For public collections (`/products`, `/categories`, etc.), `list` operations are inherently secure as all documents are publicly readable. Rules grant `read` access broadly.
*   For user-owned collections (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/addresses`, `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/orders`), the path-based ownership (`{userProfileId} == request.auth.uid`) automatically enforces QAPs. A `list` operation will only return documents whose parent path matches the authenticated user's ID, effectively pre-filtering the results by the security rules.
*   For `ProductReview` (`/products/{productId}/reviews`), rules will enforce `allow read: if resource.data.isApproved == true;` for public visibility. This ensures that `list` queries will only ever return approved reviews, upholding QAPs by not exposing unapproved content that a user is unauthorized to see, even if they explicitly query for it. Any unapproved reviews will simply not be returned by a `list` query for non-admin users, making the rules effectively filter without being actual database filters.

This structure prioritizes explicit access control, minimizes rule complexity, and enhances the overall security and maintainability of the application.
